summary:
Given the root of a binary tree, decide whether it is height-balanced and return true or false.

A tree is height-balanced when, for every node, the heights of its left and right subtrees differ by at most one.

examples.explanation: At every node the left and right subtree heights are within one of each other.
The left side grows two levels deeper than the right at the top node, breaking the balance rule.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 5000].
An empty tree is considered balanced and returns true.
